evidence: The paper ranks 7,279 proteins using random forest mean decrease in Gini index and identifies 5 candidates that rank in the top 50. However, primary candidate selection was linear regression, and AGPAT1 is substantiated through independent cohort validation (P = 0.009) and immunohistochemistry confirmation. Only 1 of 5 importance-supported candidates replicated, indicating importance ranking did not predict validation success. The core conclusion that AGPAT1 differentiates PSC-UC from UC relies on held-out validation and orthogonal methods, not on the magnitude or ranking of impurity importance scores.
